metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1005
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Authors used a pSuper-based siRNA construct to decrease contactin levels of primary hippocampal neurons and observed a significant decrease in the number of dendritic protrusions, suggesting that cortactin plays a role in spine formation or stability. The remaining spines were positive for F-actin and PSD95. By contrary, overexpression of cortactin induced an increase in spine length mainly attributable to increase in spine neck. Interestingly, this effect was mimicked by the overexpression of a cortactin mutant carrying only the NTA and tandem repeat domains of the protein, involved in actin polymerization.
Overexpression of cortactin in aspiny inhibitory neurons also induced the small protrusions resembling filopodia, supporting the role of cortactin in spine formation.

11/8/2017 Pim
- The data in this paper are rather corralational and strongly depend on the prior knowledge that "...cortactin binds to F-actin and affects actin polymerization...". However, the combined data on spine morphology and actin/cortactin localization, in my view, support the selected term 'regulation of modification of postsynaptic actin cytoskeleton (GO:1905274)'.. Experimental description: - For all constructs except W22A and W22A tandem, murine wild-type cortactin was used as template (gift of J. Thomas Par- son, University of Virginia, Charlottesville, VA). For W22A and W22A tandem the clone pJP20 was used as template.
-For the generation of the short- interfering RNA construct, DNA oligonucleotides corresponding to nucleotides 300 -318 of the rat and mouse cortactin sequence were used.
-Hippocampal neuron cultures were prepared from embryonic day 18 -19 rat embryos

17/11/2017 Pim
- The species of the Cortactin cDNA was not explicitly stated in the paper.
- rat species was selected based on the origin of the neuronal culture and the shRNA approach.
